top_categories.workout_context: 11,483ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
93ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Marcha Cicloturista Quebrantahuesos, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
At mile 60 of 123, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Marcha Cicloturista Quebrantahuesos' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
11,483ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Marcha Cicloturista Quebrantahuesos. Train it until it feels boring.
Marcha Cicloturista Quebrantahuesos' paved Pyrenean mountain passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 123 miles.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 123 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Low-cadence force work for 11,483ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved Pyrenean mountain passes. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 11,483ft of climbing on paved Pyrenean mountain passes does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Marcha Cicloturista Quebrantahuesos’s 123 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Marcha Cicloturista Quebrantahuesos. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, TT Threshold, and Over Under to prepare you for 123 miles of paved pyrenean mountain passes in Sabinanigo, Huesca, Spain.
